-{"id":"bd-3zm7","title":"bd mol advance: Step through molecule execution","description":"Implement bd mol advance for stepping through molecules.

## Command

```bash
bd mol advance <mol-id>
```

## Behavior

1. Load molecule state from .beads/molecules/<mol-id>.state.yaml
2. Find current step
3. Mark current step completed
4. Find next available step (respecting needs/deps)
5. Update state file
6. Output next step info (or COMPLETE if done)

## Step State Machine

```
pending → in_progress → completed
```

When advancing:
- Current step: in_progress → completed
- Next step: pending → in_progress

## Output

```bash
$ bd mol advance mol-deacon-patrol
✓ Step inbox-check completed
→ Next step: spawn-work

Title: Spawn polecat for ready work
Description: ...
```

Or if complete:
```bash
$ bd mol advance mol-deacon-patrol
✓ Step self-inspect completed
✓ Molecule COMPLETE

Run bd mol reset to loop, or gt handoff to cycle.
```

- cmd/bd/mol_advance.go

 {"id":"bd-411u","title":"Document BEADS_DIR pattern for multi-agent workspaces (Gas Town)","description":"Gas Town and similar multi-agent systems need to configure separate beads databases per workspace/rig, distinct from any project-level beads.

## Use Case

In Gas Town:
- Each 'rig' (managed project) has multiple agents (polecats, refinery, witness)
- All agents in a rig should share a single beads database at the rig level
- This should be separate from any .beads/ the project itself uses
- The BEADS_DIR env var enables this

## Documentation Needed

1. Add a section to docs explaining BEADS_DIR for multi-agent setups
2. Example: setting BEADS_DIR in agent startup scripts/hooks
3. Clarify interaction with project-level .beads/ (BEADS_DIR takes precedence)

## Current Support

Already implemented in internal/beads/beads.go:FindDatabasePath():
- BEADS_DIR env var is checked first (preferred)
- BEADS_DB env var still supported (deprecated)
- Falls back to .beads/ search in tree

Just needs documentation for the multi-agent workspace pattern.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T22:08:22.158027-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T22:08:22.158027-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-hobo","title":"Distinct prefixes for protos, molecules, wisps","description":"Template/workflow entities should have visually distinct prefixes from regular issues.

**Problem:**
- Protos (bd-7bs4) look like regular issues - invites squashing
- Molecules (poured instances) also use bd- prefix
- Wisps are in separate DB but still use bd- when referenced

**Proposed Prefixes:**
- `proto-` for templates (e.g., proto-release, proto-review)
- `mol-` for active molecules (poured from protos)
- `wisp-` for ephemeral wisps (vapor phase)

**Benefits:**
- Instant visual recognition of entity type
- Prevents accidental modification of templates
- Clear lifecycle: proto → mol → wisp → digest

**Implementation options:**
1. Separate databases with different prefixes
2. Issue type determines prefix generation
3. Naming convention enforced by bd pour/wisp commands","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T16:45:24.940809-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T16:45:24.940809-08:00","labels":["workflow"]}
 {"id":"bd-hr39","title":"bd cook: needs field not converted to step dependencies","description":"When cooking a formula with `needs` fields on steps, the dependencies are not created between sibling steps.

## Expected

Formula:
```yaml
steps:
  - id: inbox-check
    title: Check inbox
  - id: trigger-spawns
    title: Nudge polecats
    needs: [inbox-check]
```

Should create:
- mol-foo.inbox-check (no deps)
- mol-foo.trigger-spawns → depends on → mol-foo.inbox-check

## Actual

Both steps only depend on the parent proto:
- mol-foo.inbox-check → depends on → mol-foo
- mol-foo.trigger-spawns → depends on → mol-foo

The `needs` field is ignored.

## Impact

This breaks the step execution order. Steps that should wait for predecessors will run in parallel or out of order.

## Reproduction

```bash
bd cook mol-deacon-patrol.formula.yaml
bd show mol-deacon-patrol.trigger-pending-spawns
# Shows: Depends on mol-deacon-patrol (parent only)
# Should show: Depends on mol-deacon-patrol.inbox-check
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:49:59.023514-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.929298-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.929298-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in this session"}
-{"id":"bd-hulf","title":"Molecule execution state management","description":"Implement molecule execution state tracking.

## State Location

```
.beads/molecules/<mol-id>.state.yaml
```

## State Schema

```yaml
id: mol-deacon-patrol
formula: mol-deacon-patrol  # Source formula
bonded_at: ISO timestamp
bonded_by: entity who created it

# Execution state
status: running | paused | completed | failed
current_step: step-id
started_at: ISO timestamp
completed_at: ISO timestamp (if done)

# Loop tracking
reset_count: 0
last_reset_at: null

# Per-step state
steps:
  inbox-check:
    status: completed | in_progress | pending
    started_at: ...
    completed_at: ...
  spawn-work:
    status: pending
  self-inspect:
    status: pending

# Variables (from formula instantiation)
variables:
  rig: gastown
  issue_id: gt-xxx
```

## Operations

- CreateState(molID, formula, variables) - Initialize state
- LoadState(molID) - Read current state  
- SaveState(molID, state) - Write state
- AdvanceStep(molID) - Mark current complete, find next
- ResetState(molID) - Reset all steps to pending

## Files

- internal/mol/state.go
- internal/mol/types.go","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T15:53:43.381634-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T15:53:43.381634-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-hy9p","title":"Add --body-file flag to bd create for reading descriptions from files","description":"## Problem

Creating issues with long/complex descriptions via CLI requires shell escaping gymnastics:

```bash
# Current workaround - awkward heredoc quoting
bd create --title="..." --description="$(cat <<'EOF'
...markdown...
EOF
)"

# Often fails with quote escaping errors in eval context
# Agents resort to writing temp files then reading them
```

## Proposed Solution

Add `--body-file` and `--description-file` flags to read description from a file, matching `gh` CLI pattern.

```bash
# Natural pattern that aligns with training data
cat > /tmp/desc.md << 'EOF'
...markdown content...
EOF

bd create --title="..." --body-file=/tmp/desc.md
```

## Implementation

### 1. Add new flags to `bd create`

```go
createCmd.Flags().String("body-file", "", "Read description from file (use - for stdin)")
createCmd.Flags().String("description-file", "", "Alias for --body-file")
```

### 2. Flag precedence

- If `--body-file` or `--description-file` is provided, read from file
- If value is `-`, read from stdin
- Otherwise fall back to `--body` or `--description` flag
- If neither provided, description is empty (current behavior)

### 3. Error handling

- File doesn't exist → clear error message
- File not readable → clear error message
- stdin specified but not available → clear error message

## Benefits

✅ **Matches training data**: `gh issue create --body-file file.txt` is a common pattern
✅ **No shell escaping issues**: File content is read directly
✅ **Works with any content**: Markdown, special characters, quotes, etc.
✅ **Agent-friendly**: Agents already write complex content to temp files
✅ **User-friendly**: Easier for humans too when pasting long descriptions

## Related Commands

Consider adding similar support to:
- `bd update --body-file` (for updating descriptions)
- `bd comment --body-file` (if/when we add comments)

## Examples

```bash
# From file
bd create --title="Add new feature" --body-file=feature.md

# From stdin
echo "Quick description" | bd create --title="Bug fix" --body-file=-

# With other flags
bd create \
  --title="Security issue" \
  --type=bug \
  --priority=0 \
  --body-file=security-report.md \
  --label=security
```

## Testing

- Test with normal files
- Test with stdin (`-`)
- Test with non-existent files (error handling)
- Test with binary files (should handle gracefully)
- Test with empty files (valid - empty description)
- Test that `--description-file` and `--body-file` are equivalent aliases","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-22T00:02:08.762684-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.536024-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:28:52.505239-08:00"}
@@ -485,7 +485,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-tm2p","title":"Polecats get stuck on interactive shell prompts (cp/mv/rm -i)","description":"During swarm operations, polecats frequently get stuck waiting for interactive prompts from shell commands like:
- cp prompting 'overwrite file? (y/n)'
- mv prompting 'overwrite file? (y/n)'  
- rm prompting 'remove file?'

This happens because macOS aliases or shell configs may have -i flags set by default.

Root cause: Claude Code runs commands that trigger interactive confirmation prompts, but cannot respond to them, causing the agent to hang indefinitely.

Observed in: Multiple polecats during GH issues swarm (Dec 2024)
- Derrick, Roustabout, Prospector, Warboy all got stuck on y/n prompts

Suggested fixes:
1. AGENTS.md should instruct agents to always use -f flag with cp/mv/rm
2. Polecat startup could set shell aliases to use non-interactive versions
3. bd prime hook could include guidance about non-interactive commands
4. Consider detecting stuck prompts and auto-recovering","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-14T16:51:24.572271-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.536312-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:13:04.074424-08:00"}

-{"id":"bd-ttbf","title":"bd mol reset: Reset molecule to initial state for looping","description":"Implement bd mol reset for patrol loop behavior.

## Command

```bash
bd mol reset <mol-id>
```

## Behavior

1. Load molecule state from .beads/molecules/<mol-id>.state.yaml
2. Reset all step states to pending
3. Clear current_step (or set to first step)
4. Preserve: bonded_at, bonded_by, variables
5. Update: reset_count++, last_reset_at

## Use Case: Deacon Patrol Loop

```
inbox-check → spawn-work → self-inspect → mol-reset → (loop)
```

The Deacon runs its patrol, then resets the molecule to start fresh.
No gt handoff needed unless context is high.

## State After Reset

```yaml
id: mol-deacon-patrol
status: running
current_step: inbox-check  # Back to first
reset_count: 5
last_reset_at: 2025-12-24T...
steps:
  inbox-check: pending      # All reset
  spawn-work: pending
  self-inspect: pending
```

## Files

- cmd/bd/mol_reset.go
- internal/mol/state.go (add Reset method)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T15:53:20.154106-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T15:53:20.154106-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ttbf","depends_on_id":"bd-hulf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T15:53:49.159903-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tvu3","title":"Improve test coverage for internal/beads (48.1% → 70%)","description":"Improve test coverage for internal/beads package from 48% to 70%.

## Current State
- Coverage: 48.4%
- Files: beads.go, fingerprint.go
- Tests: beads_test.go (moderate coverage)

## Functions Needing Tests

### beads.go (database discovery)
- [ ] followRedirect - needs redirect file tests
- [ ] findDatabaseInBeadsDir - needs various dir structures
- [x] NewSQLiteStorage - likely covered
- [ ] FindDatabasePath - needs BEADS_DB env var tests
- [ ] hasBeadsProjectFiles - needs file existence tests
- [ ] FindBeadsDir - needs directory traversal tests
- [ ] FindJSONLPath - needs path derivation tests
- [ ] findGitRoot - needs git repo tests
- [ ] findDatabaseInTree - needs nested directory tests
- [ ] FindAllDatabases - needs multi-database tests
- [ ] FindWispDir - needs wisp directory tests
- [ ] FindWispDatabasePath - needs wisp path tests
- [ ] NewWispStorage - needs wisp storage tests
- [ ] EnsureWispGitignore - needs gitignore creation tests
- [ ] IsWispDatabase - needs path classification tests

### fingerprint.go (repo identification)
- [ ] ComputeRepoID - needs various remote URL tests
- [ ] canonicalizeGitURL - needs URL normalization tests
- [ ] GetCloneID - needs clone identification tests

## Implementation Guide

1. **Use temp directories:**
   ```go
   func TestFindBeadsDir(t *testing.T) {
       tmpDir := t.TempDir()
       beadsDir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, ".beads")
       os.MkdirAll(beadsDir, 0755)
       
       // Create test files
       os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(beadsDir, "beads.db"), []byte{}, 0644)
       
       // Change to tmpDir and test
       oldWd, _ := os.Getwd()
       os.Chdir(tmpDir)
       defer os.Chdir(oldWd)
       
       result := FindBeadsDir()
       assert.Equal(t, beadsDir, result)
   }
   ```

2. **Test scenarios:**
   - BEADS_DB environment variable set
   - .beads/ in current directory
   - .beads/ in parent directory
   - Redirect file pointing elsewhere
   - No beads directory found
   - Wisp directory alongside main beads

3. **Git remote URL tests:**
   ```go
   tests := []struct{
       input    string
       expected string
   }{
       {"git@github.com:user/repo.git", "github.com/user/repo"},
       {"https://github.com/user/repo", "github.com/user/repo"},
       {"ssh://git@github.com/user/repo.git", "github.com/user/repo"},
   }
   ```

## Success Criteria
- Coverage ≥ 70%
- All FindXxx functions have tests
- Environment variable handling tested
- Edge cases (missing dirs, redirects) covered

## Run Tests
```bash
